title: eremophila abietina subsp. ciliata
intro: Eremophila abietina subsp. ciliata is a subspecies of the Eremophila abietina plant, commonly known as the "Fingertip Emu Bush" due to its distinctive finger-like leaves. This plant has been used in traditional Australian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Eremophila abietina subsp. ciliata include relief from colds, flu, and respiratory issues, as well as potential anti-cancer properties. Eremophila abietina subsp. ciliata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications to treat skin conditions and wounds.
